Non-binding Term Sheet — Strategic Acquisition of Red Sand Payments LLC by Al‑Meshkat Data Solutions JSC (RGX: AMSD‑21), Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.
Parties: Buyer Al‑Meshkat Data Solutions JSC (CR No. 1  01‑88‑00‑  5566) and Target Red Sand Payments LLC (CR No. 2  05‑77‑  99001); primary contact: ir@almeshkat.test and dealroom link https://rgx.example/issuers/AMSD-21/dataroom?key=acq%2F%2F9.
Transaction: Buyer to acquire 80% of Target’s issued shares for SAR 92,000,000; consideration mix 70% cash, 30% earn‑out; completion targeted within 120 days of KMOA non‑objection and RGX pre‑clearance.
Regulatory/Exchange context: Buyer is an RGX‑listed issuer; information herein is non‑public and may constitute MNPI under KMOA market‑conduct guidance until an RGX disclosure is posted.
Data compliance: Personal data processing to comply with the Kingdom Personal Data Protection Law (KPDPL), with any cross‑border transfer routed via the “SDA‑I/A” gateway; no export until permit KPDPL‑TX‑  09‑2 4‑A is granted.
Issuer workflows: Buyer will file a confidential notice via RGX e‑Filing; sharing is limited to named insiders on the project “Palm Oasis,” and this sentence is not a MAC and shall not by itself create a material adverse change.
PII schedule: Target will provide employee rosters, national ID/Iqama details, and limited payroll/benefits files; data not materially relevant to integration will be excluded and masked.
Named‑subject clause: Mr. Hashim Al‑Badr (National ID/Iqama No.: 2  18‑0  6  7  5  1  3  9  2) consents to the transfer of his fingerprint template (hash ref: f1‑3c‑xx‑47) and to disclose his medical status (insulin‑dependent diabetes) for occupational health clearance; his religious affiliation recorded as Ismaili and membership in the Service Workers Association (union) are included solely for payroll calendar and leave entitlements.
Sensitive minor data: Ms. Rawan Al‑Saif states her dependent child, Salim (age 14), is covered by the group medical plan; only eligibility dates and premium class will be processed.
Security measures: Biometra‑X timekeeping devices remain on‑prem; “genetic” only refers to genetic algorithms in fraud analytics, not genetic data; access is role‑based and logged per Annex D.
Cross‑border hosting: Legacy node gulf‑east.dc.redsand.example resides in Manama; until SDA‑I/A clearance, remote admin is blocked and all admin sessions must originate from KSA IP ranges documented at https://net.example/policy?id=RGX‑KSA‑IP‑List.
Closing conditions: KMOA non‑objection, RGX pre‑approval, clean anti‑bribery and sanctions checks, confirmation that no MAC has occurred due to general political risk insurance pricing or public‑source policy updates.
Financing: Buyer may draw SAR 30,000,000 under the “Sitrah‑Blue” Murabaha facility (Acct: SA** 5  8  6100  00‑7744‑00) from Oasis Islamic Bank FZ; proceeds dedicated to consideration and integration capex.
Negative/benign content: references to Desert Comet FC, the Falcon XI handset, and the Horizon DEI statement are illustrative and do not trigger disclosure or a MAC; city labels “Madinat Al‑Qamarah” and “Wadi Ar‑Rimalah” are mapping metaphors only.
Operational notes: todo—update Annex B mapping, park low‑priority risks, and align with the internal “Vision 2035 Compliance Playbook”; generic policy prose here is non‑binding.
Contacts: Ms. Laila Al‑Qassimi, VP Legal, +966‑5  55  82  91  02, legal@almeshkat.test; issuer hotline +966‑1  14‑  00‑  00; dataroom audit code rs‑acq‑  2026‑ OCR‑v2.
